info.description:
Calendly’s API is [REST](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Representational_State_Transfer)-based and has predictable resource-oriented URLs. It uses **JSON** for request and response bodies and standard HTTP methods, authentication, and response codes.
##### Authenticate with personal access tokens or OAuth 2.0 To access Calendly data through the API, you can authenticate with **personal access tokens** or **OAuth 2.0**. To learn more about these authentication methods and when and how to use them, see [Getting Started with the Calendly API](https://developer.calendly.com/getting-started).
info.title: Calendly API

get.description:
Returns a list of Invitees for an event.

<!-- theme: info -->
  > #### Required scopes: `scheduled_events:read`

Returns a list of  Events.

* Pass `organization` parameter to return events for that organization (requires admin/owner privilege)
* Pass `user` parameter to return events for a specific User
* Pass `group` parameter to return events for a specific Group (requires organization admin/owner or group admin privilege)

**NOTES:**
* If you are the admin/owner of the organization, you can use both `organization` and `user` to get a list of events for a specific user within your organization.
* If you are the admin/owner of the organization, you can use both `organization` and `group` to get a list of events for a specific group within your organization.

* `user` can only be used alone when requesting your own personal events. This will return your events within any organization that you are currently in or were a part of in the past.

<!-- theme: info -->
  > #### Required scopes: `scheduled_events:read`

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Return events that are scheduled with the user associated with this URI

parameters.name: organization
parameters.description: Return events that are scheduled with the organization associated with this URI

parameters.name: invitee_email
parameters.description: Return events that are scheduled with the invitee associated with this email address

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Whether the scheduled event is `active` or `canceled`

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Order results by the specified field and direction. Accepts comma-separated list of {field}:{direction} values.
Supported fields are: start_time.
Sort direction is specified as: asc, desc.

parameters.name: min_start_time
parameters.description: Include events with start times after this time (sample time format: "2020-01-02T03:04:05.678123Z"). This time should use the UTC timezone.

parameters.name: max_start_time
parameters.description: Include events with start times prior to this time (sample time format: "2020-01-02T03:04:05.678123Z"). This time should use the UTC timezone.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Return events that are scheduled with the group associated with this URI

collection.description: The set of scheduled events matching the criteria

location.additional_info: Please check in at the main lobby.

get.description:
Returns all Event Types associated with a specified User. Use:

* `organization` to look up all Event Types that belong to the organization

* `user` to look up a user's Event Types in an organization

Either `organization` or `user` are required query params when using this endpoint.

<!-- theme: info -->
  > #### Required scopes: `event_types:read`

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: Return only active event types if true, only inactive if false, or all event types if this parameter is omitted.

parameters.name: organization
parameters.description: View available personal, team, and organization event types associated with the organization's URI.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: View available personal, team, and organization event types associated with the user's URI.

parameters.name: user_availability_schedule
parameters.description: Used in conjunction with `user` parameter, returns a filtered list of Event Types that use the given primary availability schedule.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Order results by the specified field and direction. Accepts comma-separated list of {field}:{direction} values.Supported fields are: name, position, created_at, updated_at. Sort direction is specified as: asc, desc.

withOmittedDirection.summary: Omit direction with fallback to asc by default

parameters.name: admin_managed
parameters.description: Return only admin managed event types if true, exclude admin managed event types if false, or include all event types if this parameter is omitted.

locale.description: The locale on the event type, used to determine the language of the event type's scheduling page

post.description:
Create a new Event Invitee.

Standard notifications, calendar invites, reschedules, and workflows run as if booked via the Calendly UI.

**NOTE:**
* Access to this endpoint is limited to Calendly users on paid plans (Standard and above).  Users on the Free plan will receive a 403 Forbidden response.

<!-- theme: info -->
  > #### Required scopes: `scheduled_events:write`

event_type.description: Canonical reference (unique identifier) for the event type being scheduled

start_time.description: The start time in UTC of the scheduled event

name.description: The full name of the invitee. **Required if** `first_name` **is not provided**

first_name.description: The first name of the invitee. **Required if** `name` **is not provided**

last_name.description: The last name of the invitee

text_reminder_number.description: Invitee's phone number for SMS reminders. Must be a valid phone number (e.g. +14155551234)

booking_source.description: The source or origin of the booking (e.g. ai_scheduling_assistant)

Use this to list the Organization Memberships for all users belonging to an organization, use:

* `user` to look up a user's membership in an organization

* `organization` to look up all users that belong to the organization

This endpoint can also be used to retrieve your organization URI.

post.description:
Create a Webhook Subscription for an Organization or User. * The `organization` subscription scope triggers the webhook for all subscribed events within the organization. * The `user` subscription scope only triggers the webhook for subscribed events that belong to the specific user. * The `group` subscription scope only triggers the webhook for subscribed events that belong to the specfic group. | Event | Allowed Subscription Scopes | Required Auth Scope | | ----- | ----- | ----- | | invitee.created | `organization` `user` `group` | `scheduled_events:read` | | invitee.canceled | `organization` `user` `group` | `scheduled_events:read` | | invitee_no_show.created | `organization` `user` `group` | `scheduled_events:read` | | invitee_no_show.deleted | `organization` `user` `group` | `scheduled_events:read` | | event_type.created | `organization` `user` `group` | `event_types:read` | | event_type.deleted | `organization` `user` `group` | `event_types:read` | | event_type.updated | `organization` `user` `group` | `event_types:read` | | routing_form_submission.created | `organization`
Create separate Webhook Subscriptions for events with different subscription scopes. | `routing_forms:read` | > #### Required scopes: `webhooks:write`

url.description: The URL where you want to receive POST requests for events you are subscribed to.

events.description: List of user events to subscribe to.

organization.description: The unique reference to the organization that the webhook will be tied to.

user.description: The unique reference to the user that the webhook will be tied to.

group.description: The unique reference to the group that the webhook will be tied to.

scope.description: Indicates if the webhook subscription scope will be "organization" or "user"

signing_key.description: Optional secret key shared between your application and Calendly. See https://developer.calendly.com/api-docs/ZG9jOjM2MzE2MDM4-webhook-signatures for additional information.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Indicates if the results should be filtered by group. This parameter is only required if the `scope` parameter is set to `group`.

Returns a list of available times for an event type within a specified date range.

Date range can be no greater than 1 week (7 days).

**NOTE:**
* This endpoint does not support traditional keyset pagination.

<!-- theme: info -->
  > #### Required scopes: `availability:read`

parameters.name: event_type
parameters.description: The uri associated with the event type

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Start time of the requested availability range. Date cannot be in the past.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: End time of the requested availability range. Date must be in the future of start_time.

collection.description: The set of available times for the event type matching the criteria

get.description: > This endpoint requires an Enterprise subscription. > > #### Required scopes: `activity_log:read` Returns a list of activity log entries

parameters.name: organization
parameters.description: Return activity log entries from the organization associated with this URI

parameters.name: search_term
parameters.description:
Filters entries based on the search term.

Supported operators:
  - `|` - to allow filtering by one term or another. Example: `this | that`
  - `+` - to allow filtering by one term and another. Example: `this + that`
  - `"` - to allow filtering by an exact search term. Example: `"email@website.com"`
  - `-` - to omit specific terms from results. Example: `Added -User`
  - `()` - to allow specifying precedence during a search. Example: `(this + that) OR (person + place)`
  - `*` - to allow prefix searching. Example `*@other-website.com`

basic-term.summary: A simple search term to filter with

complex-term.summary: A complex search term to filter with

parameters.name: actor
parameters.description: Return entries from the user(s) associated with the provided URIs

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Order results by the specified field and direction. List of {field}:{direction} values.

parameters.name: min_occurred_at
parameters.description: Include entries that occurred after this time (sample time format: "2020-01-02T03:04:05.678Z"). This time should use the UTC timezone.

parameters.name: max_occurred_at
parameters.description: Include entries that occurred prior to this time (sample time format: "2020-01-02T03:04:05.678Z"). This time should use the UTC timezone.

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: The token to pass to get the next portion of the collection

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: The number of rows to return

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: The categories of the entries

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The action(s) associated with the entries

collection.description: The set of activity log entries matching the criteria

last_event_time.description: The date and time of the newest entry (format: "2020-01-02T03:04:05.678Z") in the collection array.

total_count.description: Total number of records based on search criteria

exceeds_max_total_count.description: If there are more search results than the total_count field indicates, pagination will continue to return results past the total_count field value.

duration.description: Must be one of the provided duration options. If duration options aren't provided then duration must be one of the duration options inherited from the event type.

duration_options.description: A maximum of 4 unique options is allowed. Each option must be >= 1 and <= 720.

hide_location.description: determines if a location is hidden until invitee books a spot, only respected when there is a single custom location configured

location.description: is only supported when `kind` is 'physical', 'custom' or 'ask_invitee'

additional_info.description: is only supported when `kind` is 'physical' or 'inbound_call'

rules.description: are required when an availability rule is provided

timezone.description: is required when an availability rule is provided

post.description:
Endpoint for our [Customize Once and Share](https://help.calendly.com/hc/en-us/articles/10594902199191) feature. This allows you to customize events for a specific invitee without needing to make an entirely new event type.
***This feature is only available for one-on-one event types.***
**Note: Any parameter which is not provided in the request body will be copied from the target event type.**

<!-- theme: info -->
  > #### Required scopes: `shares:write`

get.description:
Returns an ascending list of user internal and external scheduled events within a specified date range.

Date range can be no greater than 1 week (7 days).

**NOTE:**
* This endpoint does not support traditional keyset pagination.
* External events will only be returned for calendars that have "Check for conflicts" configured.

<!-- theme: info -->
  > #### Required scopes: `availability:read`

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The uri associated with the user

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Start time of the requested availability range. Date cannot be in the past.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: End time of the requested availability range. Date must be in the future of start_time.

collection.description: The set of internal and external scheduled calendar events matching the criteria

post.description: > This endpoint requires an Enterprise subscription. > > #### Required scopes: `data_compliance:write` To submit a request to remove scheduled events data within a time range for your organization, use this endpoint. Requests for data deletion can take up to 7 days to complete. Time range can be no greater than 24 months and must occur in the past.

start_time.description: The scheduled events UTC timestamp at which data deletion should begin.

end_time.description: The scheduled events UTC timestamp at which data deletion should end.

requestBody.description: The start and end times that delineate the time range for scheduled events data deletion.

uri.description: Canonical reference (unique identifier) for the user

EventType.description: A configuration for an Event Type
EventType.title: Event Type

custom_questions.name: What would you like to discuss?

active.description: Indicates if the event is active or not.

admin_managed.description: Indicates if this event type is managed by an organization admin

booking_method.description: Indicates if the event type is for a poll or an instant booking

color.description: The hexadecimal color value of the event type's scheduling page

created_at.description: The moment the event type was created (e.g. "2020-01-02T03:04:05.678123Z")

deleted_at.description: The moment the event type was deleted (e.g. "2020-01-02T03:04:05.678123Z"). Since event types can be deleted but their scheduled events remain it's useful to fetch a deleted event type when you still require event type data for a scheduled event.

description_html.description: The event type's description (formatted with HTML)

description_plain.description: The event type's description (in non formatted text)

duration.description: The length of sessions booked with this event type

duration_options.description: Array of duration options. Always null for adhoc event types.

internal_note.description: Contents of a note that may be associated with the event type

kind.description: Indicates if the event type is "solo" (belongs to an individual user) or "group"

locale.description: The locale on the event type, used to determine the language of the event type's scheduling page

locations.description: Configuration information for each possible location for this Event Type

name.description: The event type name (in human-readable format)

pooling_type.description: Indicates if the event type is "round robin" (alternates between hosts) or "collective" (invitees pick a time when all participants are available) or “multi-pool” (considers availability delineated by pools of participants) or "null" (the event type doesn’t consider the availability of a group participants)

position.description: Position order of Event Type, starting with 0 (for display purposes)

scheduling_url.description: The URL of the user’s scheduling site where invitees book this event type

secret.description: Indicates if the event type is hidden on the owner's main scheduling page

slug.description: The portion of the event type's URL that identifies a specific web page (in a human-readable format)

type.description: Indicates if the event type is "AdhocEventType" (ad hoc event) or "StandardEventType" (standard event type)

updated_at.description: The moment the event type was last updated (e.g. "2020-01-02T03:04:05.678123Z")

payment.terms: sample terms of payment (up to 1,024 characters)

uri.description: Canonical reference (unique identifier) for the invitee

first_name.description: The first name of the invitee who booked the event when the event type is configured to use separate fields for first name and last name. Null when event type is configured to use a single field for name.

last_name.description: The last name of the invitee who booked the event when the event type is configured to use separate fields for first name and last name. Null when event type is configured to use a single field for name.

Once the invitee has reconfirmed the `confirmed_at` attribute will change from `null` to a timestamp that reflects when they took action.

AvailabilitySchedule.title: User Availability Schedule
AvailabilitySchedule.description: The availability schedule set by the user.

uri.description: A URI reference to this Availability Schedule.

default.description: This is the default Availability Schedule in use.

name.description: The name of this Availability Schedule.

user.description: A URI reference to a User.

timezone.description: The timezone for which this Availability Schedule is originated in.

rules.description: The rules of this Availability Schedule.

AvailabilityRule.title: Availability Rule
AvailabilityRule.description: The rules for an availability schedule.

type.description: The type of this Availability Rule; can be "wday" or a specific "date".

intervals.description: The intervals to be applied to this Rule. Each interval represents when booking a meeting is allowed. If the interval array is empty, then there is no booking availability for that day. Time is in 24h format (i.e. "17:30") and local to the timezone in the Availability Schedule.

wday.description: The day of the week for which this Rule should be applied to.

date.description: A specific date in the future that this should be applied to (i.e. "2030-12-31").

key.description: Unique identifier for each holiday. Contains country code and abbreviated reference of the holiday name

date_observed.description: Date that the holiday is observed. This is the date that availability will be affected by this holiday.

MeetingLocation.title: MeetingLocation
MeetingLocation.description: A configuration for an EventType's location

connected.description: Indicates if the location configuration is connected (only valid for web conferencing)
